products.title: 6" Calathea – Decorative Foliage Houseplant
products.body_html:
The 6-inch Calathea is a lush, eye-catching houseplant known for its beautifully patterned leaves and elegant growth habit. With larger, fuller foliage than smaller sizes, this plant makes a striking tabletop or plant-stand accent. Calatheas are loved for their unique leaf markings and subtle daily leaf movement, adding texture and life to any indoor space.

Care Guide

🌞 Light

  • Bright, indirect light

  • Avoid direct sunlight, which can scorch or fade leaves

  • Low light may reduce pattern contrast

💧 Water

  • Keep soil consistently moist, never soggy

  • Water when the top 1–2 inches of soil feel dry

  • Use filtered or distilled water when possible

🌡 Temperature & Humidity

  • Prefers warm, stable conditions

  • Ideal temperature range: 65–80°F

  • Thrives in moderate to high humidity

🌿 Soil

  • Well-draining, moisture-retentive potting mix

  • A calathea or peat-based mix works well

🍃 General Care

  • Avoid cold drafts and sudden temperature changes

  • Leaves naturally move throughout the day

  • Trim damaged leaves to maintain appearance

products.title: Tulip Bulbs – Spring Flowering Perennial Bulbs
products.body_html:
Tulip bulbs are a classic choice for adding bold, vibrant color to spring gardens, landscapes, and containers. Known for their clean, elegant blooms and wide range of colors, tulips are perfect for borders, mass plantings, and seasonal displays. These bulbs are easy to plant and create a striking spring show when planted in groups.

Planting & Care Guide

🌞 Light

  • Full sun to partial shade

  • Best flowering with at least 6 hours of sunlight

🌱 Planting

  • Plant in fall before the first hard frost

  • Plant pointed end up

  • Depth: 6–8 inches

  • Spacing: 4–6 inches apart

💧 Water

  • Water well after planting

  • Keep soil lightly moist during growth

  • Avoid overwatering, especially in poorly draining soil

🌡 Temperature & Climate

  • Require a cold period to bloom

  • Best suited for temperate climates

  • In warm regions, pre-chilling bulbs may be needed

🌿 Soil

  • Well-draining soil is essential

  • Sandy or loamy soil works best

  • Avoid soggy or compacted soil

🌸 After Bloom Care

  • Remove spent flowers after blooming

  • Allow foliage to yellow naturally before cutting back

  • Bulbs may be treated as annuals in warmer climates

products.title: Daffodil Bulbs – Spring Flowering Perennial Bulbs 10PCS
products.body_html:
Bulk daffodil bulbs are an easy and reliable way to add bright spring color to gardens, landscapes, and containers. Known for their cheerful yellow blooms and strong performance, daffodils return year after year once established. Buying in bulk is ideal for mass plantings, borders, naturalized areas, or seasonal retail displays. These bulbs are hardy, low-maintenance, and well suited for beginner and experienced gardeners alike.

Planting & Care Guide

🌞 Light

  • Full sun to partial shade

  • Best flowering occurs with at least 6 hours of sunlight daily

🌱 Planting

  • Plant bulbs in fall before the ground freezes

  • Plant pointed end up

  • Depth: 4–6 inches deep

  • Spacing: 4–6 inches apart

💧 Water

  • Water thoroughly after planting

  • Keep soil lightly moist during active growth

  • Reduce watering after flowering as foliage begins to die back

🌡 Temperature & Hardiness

  • Cold-hardy and frost tolerant

  • Suitable for most temperate climates

  • Requires a cold period to bloom properly

🌿 Soil

  • Well-draining soil is essential

  • Avoid heavy, waterlogged soil

  • Garden soil amended with compost works well

🌸 After Bloom Care

  • Leave foliage intact until it yellows naturally

  • Do not cut leaves early, as they store energy for next season

  • Bulbs will multiply over time with proper care

products.body_html:
The 6-inch Bromeliad is a bold tropical houseplant prized for its vibrant flower bracts, glossy foliage, and long-lasting color. With a fuller size and stronger presence than smaller bromeliads, this plant makes an excellent statement piece for tabletops, plant stands, offices, and bright indoor spaces. Easy to care for and visually striking, bromeliads bring a clean, modern tropical look to any environment.

Care Guide

🌞 Light

  • Bright, indirect light

  • Avoid harsh direct sunlight, which can scorch leaves

  • Insufficient light may reduce color intensity

💧 Water

  • Water when the top 1–2 inches of soil feel dry

  • Keep soil lightly moist, never soggy

  • Keep a small amount of clean water in the center cup (tank)

  • Refresh cup water weekly; use filtered or distilled water if possible

🌡 Temperature & Humidity

  • Prefers warm indoor temperatures

  • Ideal range: 65–85°F

  • Enjoys moderate to high humidity but adapts well indoors

🌿 Soil

  • Well-draining potting mix

  • Bromeliad or orchid-style mixes work best

🌸 Bloom & General Care

  • Colorful bracts can last for several months

  • After blooming, the main plant will slowly decline and produce pups

  • Pups can be separated once they reach about one-third the size of the parent

products.body_html: Amazon frogbit (Limnobium laevigatum) is a floating aquatic plant with round, vibrant green leaves. The plant's fine roots provide shelter for small fish and absorb excess nutrients, acting as a natural filtration system. With its aesthetic appeal and ecological benefits, Amazon frogbit enhances the beauty and health of aquatic environments.

products.title: Azolla – Floating Water Fern for Ponds & Aquariums
products.body_html:
Enhance your pond and aquarium with Azolla, also known as the Mosquito Fern. This small floating plant forms a dense mat of green foliage (often turning red in cooler weather) that provides beauty and function in both outdoor and indoor setups.

In ponds, Azolla offers shade, helps control algae, and creates a natural habitat for fish. In aquariums, it provides surface cover for shrimp, fry, and small fish while naturally improving water quality. Its fast growth and easy care make it a favorite for aquascaping and water gardening alike.

Key Features:

  • 🌿 Floating fern – Forms a dense carpet on the water’s surface.

  • 🐟 Fish & shrimp friendly – Great cover for fry, shrimp, and small fish in aquariums.

  • 🌱 Algae control – Reduces light penetration, suppressing unwanted algae.

  • 🍂 Seasonal color – Turns reddish in cooler temps for extra visual appeal.

  • 💧 Versatile – Suitable for ponds, water gardens, tubs, and aquariums.

Care Instructions:

  • Place directly on water surface; Azolla floats freely.

  • Prefers moderate to bright light.

  • Scoop out extra growth as needed—it multiplies quickly.

  • Outdoor ponds: Hardy in warm weather; may die back in frost.

  • Aquariums: Best in tanks with gentle surface flow; trim to prevent surface overgrowth.

What You’ll Get:

  • 1 starter portion of live Azolla, packaged safely for pond or aquarium use.

  • Grown pesticide-free and safe for all aquatic life.

✨ Give your pond and aquarium a lush, natural touch with Azolla!

Perfect for bog edges, stream banks, and shallow pond margins, Water Mint is both ornamental and functional. Its dense growth helps with natural filtration, while its scent and blooms make it a favorite for wildlife-friendly gardens.

  • Temperature: Keep above 50 °F (10 °C); protect from frost

Perfect for collectors and foliage lovers, this easy-care plant adds rich, vivid color and texture wherever it grows.

products.title: 6" Jurassic™ Watermelon Rex Begonia  – Striking Foliage Plant for Shade & Indoors
products.body_html:
Bring bold color and texture to your home or shaded garden with the Jurassic™ Watermelon Rex Begonia. Its large, showy leaves feature rosy-pink centers, deep green edges, and splashes of silvery white, bordered by rich burgundy—reminiscent of a watermelon’s hues. This compact, mounded plant thrives in bright, indirect light or deep shade, making it perfect for indoor displays, containers, and shaded garden beds.

  • Height: 16–20″

  • Spread: 18–20″

  • Light: Bright indirect to deep shade

  • Water: Keep soil evenly moist, allow slight drying between waterings

  • Temperature: Keep above 50 °F (10 °C); not frost-hardy

Ideal for adding a tropical, eye-catching touch to any collection, this easy-care foliage plant is sure to be a conversation piece.

products.title: Perry’s Fire Opal Hardy Water Lily – Brilliant Fuchsia-Pink Blooms for Garden Ponds
products.body_html:
Light up your pond with the electrifying color of Perry’s Fire Opal. This hardy water lily produces large, luminous fuchsia-pink flowers with a glowing yellow center that stand out against lush green lily pads. Perfect for adding bold color to your aquatic garden, Perry’s Fire Opal is easy to grow and blooms generously from late spring into fall.

🪷 Care Guide:

  • Sunlight: Full sun (6 or more hours daily)

  • Planting Depth: 6–24 inches below water surface

  • Blooming Season: Late spring through early fall

  • Water Temperature: Prefers 70–80°F

  • Hardiness Zone: USDA zones 4–10

  • Tips: Plant in a wide, shallow container using aquatic planting media or heavy clay soil. Fertilize monthly during growing season for maximum blooms.

✨ A vibrant focal point that turns any pond into a living masterpiece.

products.title: Peaches & Cream Hardy Water Lily – Fragrant Blooms with Creamy Peach Petals
products.body_html:
Transform your pond into a paradise with the Peaches & Cream Hardy Water Lily. This elegant aquatic plant features creamy petals kissed with peach tones and a warm golden center. Ideal for any size water garden, it blooms repeatedly throughout the season and provides shade for fish while helping reduce algae growth.

🪷 Care Guide:

  • Sunlight: Full sun (6+ hours daily)

  • Depth: Plant 6–24 inches below the water surface

  • Blooming Season: Late spring to early fall

  • Water Temp: Best in 70–80°F

  • Zone: Hardy in USDA zones 4–10

  • Tips: Use a wide, shallow container with heavy clay soil or aquatic planting media. Fertilize monthly during the growing season.

✨ Fragrant, vibrant, and low-maintenance — Peaches & Cream is a must-have centerpiece for your pond.

BERIT STRAWN Hardy Water Lily – Live Aquatic Pond Plant is a stunning hardy water lily perfect for ponds, water gardens, and container water features. Known for its vibrant blooms and floating foliage, this lily is a favorite among aquatic plant enthusiasts.

**Care Guide:**
- **Light:** Full sun (at least 5–6 hours daily)
- **Water Depth:** 6–18 inches above the crown
- **Bloom Time:** Spring to Fall
- **Hardiness Zones:** USDA 3–10
- **Planting Tips:** Use aquatic soil or a container, and place in still water.

Ideal for beginners and seasoned pond keepers alike!

products.title: Selaginella Moss 2"– Spikemoss / Clubmoss / Resurrection Plant

Also known as:

  • Spikemoss

  • Clubmoss

  • Resurrection Plant (for certain varieties like Selaginella lepidophylla)

  • Selaginella kraussiana (common species)

  • Frosty Fern (nickname for some holiday varieties)

Care Guide:

  • Light: Low to medium indirect light

  • Water: Keep soil consistently moist – never let it dry out

  • Humidity: Requires high humidity (great for terrariums or humid rooms)

  • Growth Habit: Creeping, mat-forming

  • Toxicity: Non-toxic (pet-friendly!)

Perfect for terrariums, fairy gardens, and lush understory displays, Selaginella Moss is an eye-catching addition to any collection that loves moisture and shade.

🛒 Available now at enchantedgrow.com – Bring ancient green magic into your modern home.

products.title: Colorado Hardy Water Lily - Vibrant Pink Aquatic Pond Plant
products.body_html:
The Colorado Water Lily is a standout choice for creating a captivating pond or water garden. It produces an abundance of blooms throughout the growing season and is highly versatile, thriving in a wide range of pond sizes. The Colorado Water Lily not only enhances the aesthetic of your pond but also contributes to a balanced ecosystem by providing shade and oxygenation.

Care Guide:

  • Light: Prefers full sun for consistent and vibrant blooming.
  • Water Depth: Plant at a depth of 12-24 inches for optimal growth.
  • Fertilization: Apply aquatic plant fertilizer every 4-6 weeks during the growing season.
  • Maintenance: Trim away spent blooms and leaves to maintain a healthy and clean pond environment.

Bring a splash of bold, natural beauty to your water garden with the Colorado Water Lily. Ideal for beginners and pond enthusiasts alike!
